items.title: Bank of Canada Surveys Show War Boosted Inflation Expectations, Investment

items.title: Saudis Slash Main Oil Price to Rare Discount as Market Dives
items.summary: Saudi Arabia made big reductions to its main crude oil prices for buyers in Asia, selling barrels at a discount for the first time since it embarked on a price war in 2020, as a surge of global supply heightens competition to find buyers.

items.title: Iran Will Stay in Control of Hormuz, Treyz Says
items.summary: Henrietta Treyz, Veda Partners director of economic policy research, discussed expectations for the upcoming NATO summit, oil prices and how Iran will stay in control of the Strait of Hormuz after the war is over. She speaks on "Bloomberg Surveillance." (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: With a Fragile Ceasefire under Threat, What Future for the Strait of Hormuz?
items.summary: The U.S.-Israeli war with Iran prompted Tehran to close the Strait of Hormuz, allowing only selected ships to pass through. In this Q&A, Crisis Group experts Yasmine Farouk, Chris Newton and Ali Vaez consider what could come next in this vital maritime trade corridor.

items.title: The Funeral of Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei
items.summary: Iran’s new leaders are commemorating Ayatollah Khamenei, who was killed on the first day of the U.S.-Israeli war against the country, with a funeral unlike any other in the country’s recent history.

Tech Leads Stock Futures Higher; Trump Heads to NATO Summit | Bloomberg Brief 07/06/2026
items.summary: US equity futures rise in a tech rebound as SK Hynix is set for a US listing this week. Oil slumps as flows through the Strait of Hormuz are restored after some tanker U-turns and after OPEC+ members agree to a modest rise in oil supply next month. Presidents Donald Trump and Volodymyr Zelenskyy are expected to meet at a NATO summit in Turkey. Anneka Treon of ING discusses the earnings environment

items.title: EU should think twice before weaponising its market against China
items.summary:
For much of the post-Cold-War era, the European Union understood economic interdependence as a source of mutual prosperity. Open markets, free trade and multilateral rules were regarded not merely as economic principles but as the foundations of European normative influence. That assumption is rapidly giving way to a different logic.

Sweden was urged to prevent such incidents from recurring.

items.title: A Long Gulf War Can Starve the World. A Hormuz Transit Deal Could Save Millions.
EU and NATO Members Grapple with War in Iran
items.summary: This week on War & Peace, Olga speaks with Crisis Group’s head of EU Affairs, Lisa Musiol, about how the EU, its members and European members of NATO have responded to the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran and what it reveals about EU foreign policy and its constraints.
